deltas.releaseNoteText: `VeltInlineCommentsSection` / `velt-inline-comments-section` now supports message truncation via `messageTruncation` / `message-truncation` (default: `false`) and `messageTruncationLines` / `message-truncation-lines` (default: `4`). Truncated messages show a **Show more** button; each comment tracks its own expand/collapse state. The Show more and Show less buttons are full wireframe primitives for complete visual customization.
deltas.summary: Document messageTruncation (boolean, default false) and messageTruncationLines (number, default 4) props on VeltInlineCommentsSection, including Show more / Show less wireframe primitives

details.signature:
messageTruncation?: boolean  // default: false
messageTruncationLines?: number  // default: 4

parameters.name: messageTruncation
parameters.description: Enables per-comment message truncation with expand/collapse state

parameters.name: messageTruncationLines
parameters.description: Number of lines to show before truncating

deltas.releaseNoteText: `VeltInlineCommentsSection` / `velt-inline-comments-section` now supports message truncation via `messageTruncation` / `message-truncation` (default: `false`) and `messageTruncationLines` / `message-truncation-lines` (default: `4`). Truncated messages show a **Show more** button; each comment tracks its own expand/collapse state. The Show more and Show less buttons are full wireframe primitives for complete visual customization.
deltas.summary: Document VeltInlineCommentsSectionShowMore and VeltInlineCommentsSectionShowLess as new wireframe primitives for truncated message controls inside VeltInlineCommentsSection

deltas.releaseNoteText: Edit drafts are now preserved in memory when a user dismisses the edit composer without submitting (via click-outside, dialog destroy, or back navigation). The collapsed thread card shows the pending draft with a `(DRAFT)` badge and italic styling; clicking it re-opens the edit composer pre-filled with the saved changes. Drafts are cleared on successful submit, explicit Escape, or page refresh.
deltas.summary: Document that edit drafts are now session-preserved on dismiss (click-outside, dialog destroy, back navigation), shown as (DRAFT) badge on collapsed thread card, and cleared on submit, Escape, or page refresh — no API surface, pure behavioral change
deltas.ambiguityNote: No new API props or methods — purely behavioral. Only worth adding a rule if the skill covers observable UI behavior patterns that developers depend on. The (DRAFT) badge and pre-fill are purely automatic, but the session-only scoping (cleared on refresh) is an important constraint developers should know. Recommend a short behavioral note rather than a full rule.

skippedItems.item: Rewriter — enableDefaultUI() / disableDefaultUI() methods on RewriterElement
skippedItems.reason: No velt-rewriter-best-practices library exists in the current agent-skills bundle. Cannot land as a delta to any existing library. See disposition note below.

skippedItems.item: Rewriter — RewriterAskAiRequest.model type changed from string to AiModel union
skippedItems.reason: No velt-rewriter-best-practices library exists. AiModel is a type on the Rewriter API, not on any comments/crdt/notifications surface. Cannot land in an existing library without misclassification.

skippedItems.item: Comments bug fix — text-comment toolbar position clamping measures from .velt-text-comment-toolbar--container instead of outer host
skippedItems.reason: Internal positioning/layout fix. No API or behavior change visible to developers. No rule warranted.
skippedItems.disposition: SKIPPED — internal layout fix, no developer-facing change

skippedItems.item: Comments bug fix — new comment pin contexts seeded with cached unreadCommentsMap instead of empty default
skippedItems.reason: Fixes incorrect initial state of unread badges on newly rendered pins. This is an observable behavior change (badges now correct immediately) but it corrects a bug to match already-documented intended behavior. No new API. Not rule-worthy — the documented behavior was always 'unread badges reflect current state'; the bug was an implementation gap.
skippedItems.disposition: SKIPPED — bug fix restoring already-documented behavior, no rule change needed

skippedItems.item: Comments bug fix — @name with trailing space and no match closes panel and converts to mention chip
skippedItems.reason: Fixes @mention fallback behavior to match the documented 'new contact' fallback click behavior. This is a behavioral consistency fix. The docs for mention/autocomplete already describe this flow; the bug was a divergence from it. Not rule-worthy.
skippedItems.disposition: SKIPPED — bug fix aligning implementation with existing documented behavior
